records.summary: Atlas needs a framework capability terrain that answers what Aurelia can do, how app authors express it, where the choice is local or global, and what semantic-runtime already models.
records.rationale: App-builder lowering exposed a granularity error by treating convention, decorator metadata, and inline/local custom elements as co-equal choices. The raw evidence already exists across framework resources, corpus, router, observation, discovery, and auLink, but it is not available as one compact terrain for app-builder and future MCP capability answers. A curated framework.capabilities lens should prevent app-builder axes from being inferred from local enum guesses or isolated docs snippets.
guidance: Build `framework.capabilities` as an Atlas lens before further durable app-builder lowering-axis expansion. It should start curated and source-backed, then join existing framework/resource/router/observation/corpus/auLink evidence rather than using broad text heuristics as row identity.

Each capability row should distinguish framework concept, app-author source form, locality, policy nature, prerequisites, consequences, recommendation posture, semantic-runtime coverage, app-builder implication, and evidence.
Use locality as a first-class dimension: app-global, package/global registration, resource-local, template-local, area-local, binding-site, and route-local are not interchangeable.

Use policy nature as a first-class dimension: convention/default, explicit metadata, programmatic definition, local compression, feature or plugin admission, and semantic consequence should not be collapsed into one option enum.
Treat `auLink` as a coverage and grounding signal for semantic-runtime understanding, not as the only proof that a framework capability exists.

Start v1 from semantic-runtime-understood areas: resource/source forms and local templates, style/Shadow DOM policies, router admission and area navigation, state/observation/binding, template controllers/composition, and DI/configuration/plugin admissions.

App-builder should derive Aurelia lowering axes from this terrain. Convention-vs-explicit is app/project policy, decorator/static/define are source metadata forms, and inline custom elements are locality/code-economy choices.
Router admission can be app-global, but list/detail/menu area selection is area-local; nested or named viewports are consequences of router-driven area composition, not standalone app-global taste.

Observation capability should inform low-boilerplate app-builder guidance: direct state/domain template reads, object handoff, ID handoff, proxy observation, ordinary getter observation, and @computed metadata are separate modeling choices.
Do not use framework bundles such as StandardConfiguration as the ontology. Bundles are discovery and admission conveniences; the capabilities carried by bundles should remain visible independently.

anchors.summary: The future framework.capabilities lens should become a first-class Atlas lens id when implemented.
Existing framework discovery capability vocabulary for observation/reactivity.
Existing framework discovery capability vocabulary for router entities.
Existing framework discovery capability vocabulary for expression/parser entities.
Existing framework discovery capability vocabulary for rendering/lifecycle structures.
Existing package-export capability vocabulary used by framework discovery and bundle analysis.
Framework resource convergence evidence for built-ins, source carriers, admission, syntax, and materialization.
Docs/tests corpus evidence for app-author source forms such as local templates, styles, router, bindings, and plugins.
Router/viewport/route-context/route-tree relationship evidence for admission and area navigation capabilities.
Observation and binding observer evidence for proxy/getter/collection/binding capability rows.
Semantic-runtime coverage signal for framework concepts already mirrored through auLink.
Current app-builder lowering axes that should be rechecked against the framework capability terrain.
